name: MiniMod Vintage Transistor Core VCO
description: Discrete all-transistor Moog Model D VCO emulation. Matched PNP expo converter and P-FET buffer, four simultaneous outputs including Sharktooth, CV-controllable PWM, hard sync, LFO mode. Normalled 1V/Oct from bus.

controls.name: Octave Switch
controls.description: Rotary selector for preset octave ranges. LO setting drops frequency into LFO range for modulation use.

controls.name: TUNE
controls.description: Fine tune control. Approximately ±8 semitone range around the selected octave.
controls.detail: ±8 notes · multi-turn trim behind panel for coarse set

controls.name: EXP CV
controls.description: Attenuator for the Exp CV input. Ranges 0-120%, allowing overmodulation of the exponential core.

controls.name: LIN CV
controls.description: Attenuator for the Linear CV input. Modulates pitch subtly through the P-FET buffer stage.

controls.name: SYNC LEVEL
controls.description: Threshold/sensitivity for hard sync. Adjusts how much of the sync source reaches the core. Position 2–4 is typical with another MiniMod VCO as source.

controls.name: PWM CV
controls.description: Attenuator for PWM CV input. A triangle LFO at full gives classic sweeping PWM.

controls.name: PWM SHAPE
controls.description: Manual pulse-width control. Noon = 50% duty cycle. Remains active under PWM CV as offset.

controls.name: Sync On/Off Switch (S1/S2)
controls.description: Toggle to quickly enable/disable hard sync. S2 (down) = sync active; S1 (up) = bypassed. S1-triggered sync mode is no longer supported.
controls.detail: S1 = off · S2 = hard sync on

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: Hard sync input. Rising ramp (saw) of ±5V resets the core waveform. Falling ramps need inversion to trigger reliably.

inputs.name: 1 V/OCT
inputs.description: Primary pitch input at exactly 1 volt per octave. Normalled to the Eurorack power bus CV line; patching a cable defeats the normalled signal.

inputs.name: EXP-CV
inputs.description: Exponential modulation input. Behaves like 1V/Oct but attenuated via Exp CV knob. Accepts ±5V.

inputs.name: LIN-CV
inputs.description: Linear modulation input. Through-zero style but not TZFM; produces subtle pitch modulation up to ±7 semitones.

inputs.name: PWM-CV
inputs.description: PWM control voltage input for the square output duty cycle.

outputs.name: Shark
outputs.description: Sharktooth waveform from the original Model D. Unique spectral character sitting between saw and triangle.

outputs.name: Square
outputs.description: Square / pulse wave output with duty cycle set by PWM Shape and PWM CV.

behaviors.name: Bus-normalled 1V/Oct
behaviors.description: Picks up the distribution-bus CV line if the case routes one (e.g., from a MIDI/CV converter). Patching any cable defeats the normal. Lets MiniMod voices track a master controller without visible cabling, matching the MiniMod system design.

behaviors.name: Four simultaneous waveforms
behaviors.description: SAW, Triangle, Shark, and Square outputs all emit at once. Sharktooth is the distinctive Model D waveform, a softened saw with a triangular down-slope unique to this core. Useful as a built-in waveshaper menu via the patch cable.

behaviors.name: LO switch = LFO mode
behaviors.description: Drops the core into LFO range. With -5V at the 1V/Oct input the VCO reaches approximately 0.02Hz (one cycle per 50 seconds). All four waveform outputs remain available, making it a full-waveform LFO.

behaviors.name: Warm-up drift
behaviors.description: Vintage analog core takes ~15 minutes to stabilize. Tuning before warm-up drifts noticeably. After stabilization pitch holds better than the original Model D due to modern SMT expo converter.

behaviors.name: High-frequency tracking loss
behaviors.description: The vintage core design does not maintain 1V/Oct law above ~10kHz. Tracking flattens at the top. Matches the original Model D behavior — relevant if used as a modulation source at high rates rather than at pitch.

behaviors.name: Hard sync threshold
behaviors.description: Sync reset is level-sensitive, set by Sync Threshold. Start fully CCW and turn up gradually. Classic 'laser harp' ripping sync sounds emerge when a swept slave VCO is synced to a fixed master. Falling ramps need inversion; saw outputs from other MiniMod VCOs are ideal.

behaviors.name: PWM Shape as CV offset
behaviors.trigger: PWM CV patched while PWM Shape is adjusted
behaviors.description: Under CV control the manual Shape knob becomes an offset. Lets the user center the PWM sweep — e.g., set Shape to 30% then modulate, so the duty cycle wobbles asymmetrically around that base width.

patchIdeas.name: Classic Model D voice
patchIdeas.patch: Saw to ladder filter · 8' octave · Envelope into filter cutoff · keyboard V/oct via bus · thick monosynth bass matching the original design intent
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO:
* Octave: 8'

Filter:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO (Saw) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: PWM string pad
patchIdeas.patch: Square out · slow triangle LFO into PWM-CV at ~70% · PWM Shape at noon centered · soft pad character with continuously shifting width
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO:
* Octave: 8'
* PWM CV: 70%
* PWM SHAPE: noon

LFO:

- LFO (Tri) >> VCO (PWM-CV) // classic PWM sweep
- VCO (Square) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual-VCO hard sync lead
patchIdeas.patch: Master VCO free-running · slave VCO Sync Switch = S2 · master Saw to slave SYNC · slave frequency swept by envelope · screaming sync lead
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO 1 (master):
* Octave: 8'

VCO 2 (slave):
* Octave: 8'
* Sync Switch: S2
* SYNC LEVEL: 3

Envelope:

- VCO 1 (Saw) -> VCO 2 (SYNC)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCO 2 (EXP-CV)
- VCO 2 (Saw) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: LFO with four waveforms
patchIdeas.patch: Octave switch = LO · use Saw, Triangle, Square, Shark outs as four independent modulation sources from one oscillator · Shark gives a unique near-triangle curve unavailable on most LFOs
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO:
* Octave: LO

Filter:

VCA:

Delay:

Mixer:

- VCO (Triangle) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Square) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Shark) >> Delay (Time)
- VCO (Saw) >> Mixer (Pan CV)

patchIdeas.name: Sharktooth bass
patchIdeas.patch: Shark output into filter · tuned to 32' for sub · the Shark waveform sits between saw and triangle harmonically giving a warm, punchy low end without the buzz of pure saw
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO:
* Octave: 32'

Filter:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO (Shark) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Exp overmodulation FM
patchIdeas.patch: VCO B (modulator) into EXP-CV · EXP CV attenuator at 120% (past unity) · dirty exponential FM with aliased harmonic crashes well beyond standard FM depth
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO A:
* EXP CV: 120% (max)

VCO B:
* Octave: 8'

- VCO B (Triangle) >> VCO A (EXP-CV) // overmodulated FM
- VCO A (Saw) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Linear vibrato on bus
patchIdeas.patch: Bus-normalled V/oct from MIDI interface · small triangle LFO into LIN-CV · LIN CV attenuator low · gentle ±semitone vibrato without detuning the main pitch reference
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCO:
* Octave: 8'
* LIN CV: ~20%

LFO:

- LFO (Tri) >> VCO (LIN-CV) // subtle vibrato
- VCO (Saw) -> Filter (In)
